description:
Advances: mutagen.coverage, mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
- C1 spike at `test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs`: 4 fixture modules
  (`order_submitter.ex`, `order_processor.ex` with `use GenServer`,
  `renderer.ex` with hand-rolled `__using__/1` registering attributes +
  callbacks, `encodable.ex` with `defimpl`) under
  `test/fixtures/spike_fixture/`. Test runs 100 restore cycles and asserts:
  (a) `:code.which(<mod>)` returns a non-`cover_compiled` value after cover
  stop, (b) zero leftover cover ETS, (c) `Module.get_attribute/2` deltas
  identical to fresh-compile baseline, (d) bytecode-mutated test fails,
  (e) restored test passes.
- C2 spike at `test/mutagen_ex/integration/c2_test.exs`: fixture
  `setup_all` with ETS + named GenServer + `Application.put_env(:spike_fixture,
  :rate_limit, 100)`. Test asserts `failures: 0` every iteration of 100
  consecutive `ExUnit.run/0` invocations, `Process.list/0` grows by ≤ 50,
  `:erlang.memory(:total)` stays ≤ 1.5× baseline.
- Both tagged `@tag :spike` and `@tag :integration` so they can be excluded
  from fast-feedback loops but run by default.
- The fixture project `mix.exs` and any supporting `test_helper.exs` for the
  fixture's own ExUnit configuration live alongside the fixtures.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test --only spike`. Covers spec-verification stubs
  `mutagen.coverage.v4`, `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v3`, `.v4`.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: invariants in
  [mutagen.coverage](.spec/specs/coverage.spec.md) r3, r7 and
  [mutagen.mutation_pipeline](.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md) r6, r10,
  r11.
- Negative outcome on ANY of the 4 modules or the 100-iter loop:
  ESCALATE TO USER. Do not silently scope-restrict. The fallback architecture
  decision (child-BEAM per mutation) is a value judgment; the implementer
  must surface the specific failure mode and let the user pick (i) pivot to
  fallback for v1, (ii) scope-restrict (refuse the failing class of module),
  or (iii) cancel v1.

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/**` (this stage is test-only; the harness modules ship in S5)
- `test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs`
- Any non-spike test file

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/integration/c2_test.exs`
- `test/fixtures/spike_fixture/**`
- `test/test_helper.exs` (if adding `:spike` tag exclusion logic)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not implement `CoverageRunner`, `MutationRunner`, or any production
  module. The spike validates feasibility, not production code.
- Do not write the lane fixture (`test/fixtures/lane_project/`) here — that
  is S7.
- Resist the urge to make the spike pass at all costs. A spike failure is
  the project's most valuable signal.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [ ] `mix test --only spike` passes (or has been escalated per failure policy)
- [ ] Spec verification passes (`mutagen.coverage.v4`, `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v3`, `.v4`)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
title: S2: Spike (C1 + C2) as ExUnit integration tests — GATING
